title: Appetizers
content:
APPETIZERS

Purpose: Stimulate appetite; should have sparkle, attractiveness, pungent flavor.

Types:
Hors d'Oeuvres: Relishes—pickles, olives, stuffed celery, deviled eggs, radishes, pickled onions, vegetables.

Cocktails: Fruit pieces, juices, carbonated beverages, fish/seafood with highly seasoned sauce. Served with crisp crackers or thin dry toast.

Canapes: White, rye, or brown bread cut small, trimmed in various shapes (circles, squares, diamonds, etc.), toasted or fried in deep fat. Spread with well-seasoned combinations: tart jelly, seasoned cheese, fish, caviar, anchovy paste, eggs, pickles. Small amount; spread unevenly; garnish with brightly colored food in unusual shapes.

Service: May be served in living room before meal or dining room before/after seating. Cold appetizers thoroughly chilled; may be served with ice. Hot appetizers served hot (not warm), usually after guests seated. Cocktail napkins (small, linen, decorative) for appetizers served before table. At table: appetizer glass underlined with small plate on service plate.
